decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate to quickly bring back powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces needed to bring out these creatures from the graveyard at instant speed, overwhelming the opponent with unexpected threats. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Thoughtseize and Force of Negation, as well as protection with cards like Solitude and Teferi, Time Raveler. Ultimately, the deck aims to create explosive turns and outpace the opponent with its efficient reanimation strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly cycle through the deck and fill up the graveyard with creatures, then use cards like Violent Outburst to cast Living End and bring all those creatures back onto the battlefield. The key strategy is to control the board early on with disruptive creatures like Curator of Mysteries and Subtlety, while setting up the combo to overwhelm the opponent with a sudden swarm of creatures. The deck also includes counterspells and disruption to protect the combo and ensure its success.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively fill the graveyard with cards in order to cheat out big threats like Hollow One and Detective's Phoenix. Cards like Burning Inquiry, Faithless Looting, and Street Wraith help enable this strategy, while Nethergoyf benefits from the graveyard being filled. Lightning Bolt and Marauding Mako provide additional removal and pressure on the opponent. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and powerful threats by utilizing the graveyard effectively.

This Jeskai Aggro deck focuses on quick, aggressive plays with a mix of creature and spell-based threats. Utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Quantum Riddler, the deck aims to apply pressure early in the game and maintain board presence. Support cards such as Galvanic Discharge and Solitude offer removal options, while cards like Voice of Victory and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provide additional ways to close out the game quickly. The deck also includes versatile cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer to provide flexibility and resilience in various matchups.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. It utilizes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to put pressure on the opponent while also having removal and disruption with cards like Goblin Bombardment and Static Prison. The deck also includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Boromir, Warden of the Tower for additional support and buffs. The goal of the deck is to maintain a fast-paced offensive strategy to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quick and aggressive creature plays, paired with powerful support cards like Goblin Bombardment and Guide of Souls to control the battlefield. The inclusion of powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah adds additional pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer for card advantage and board presence. The strategy of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creatures and versatile removal spells to maintain control of the game and secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to ramp up mana quickly using cards like Utopia Sprawl and Eldrazi Temple, in order to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths. The deck also features control elements such as Kozilek's Return and Malevolent Rumble to deal with threats from the opponent. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with big creatures and efficient removal spells, while also utilizing Eldrazi synergy to create a powerful board presence and ultimately win the game.

The deck revolves around using cards like Faithless Looting, Thoughtseize, and Unearth to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones. These creatures can then be brought back onto the battlefield with Persist and other reanimation cards, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats early in the game. Additionally, the deck includes disruption in the form of Fatal Push and Spell Pierce to control the board and protect the combo pieces.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to overwhelm the opponent. It also utilizes spells like Galvanic Discharge and Voice of Victory to deal damage efficiently. The inclusion of cards like Mana Tithe and Ranger-Captain of Eos helps with disruption and maintaining board control. The deck aims to apply constant pressure and close out the game swiftly with a combination of creatures and burn spells. Additionally, cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison offer additional disruption to slow down the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays utilizing a combination of efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with burn spells such as Galvanic Discharge and Sundering Eruption to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Goblin Bombardment and Voice of Victory to provide additional reach and close out games quickly. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Haliya, Guided by Light adds flexibility and resilience to the deck, allowing for multiple paths to victory.

This Boros Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls. The deck also has a mix of burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to clear the way for attacks. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Haliya, Guided by Light provide additional power and resilience. The deck's strong mana base and utility lands like Arena of Glory and Dalkovan Encampment ensure consistency and flexibility in combat. Overall, this deck seeks to quickly establish board presence and close out games before the opponent can stabilize.

This Boros Ponza Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through land destruction, removal, and control elements. By using cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Field of Ruin to disrupt the opponent's mana base, combined with removal spells like Path to Exile and Solitude, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while building up its own resources. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion adds an additional layer of synergy and consistency to the deck's overall strategy.

This deck focuses on aggressive creatures with the ability to blink or flicker them to maximize their effectiveness. Utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck can trigger powerful enter-the-battlefield effects multiple times. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By combining aggression with powerful effects through blinking, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the combo of Goryo's Vengeance and Griselbrand to quickly reanimate a big threat and win the game. The deck includes many ways to protect this combo, such as Force of Negation and Thoughtseize. Additionally, it includes cards like Ephemerate and Psychic Frog for additional value and card advantage. The deck aims to quickly reanimate threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan, making it a powerful and versatile combo deck in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By effectively managing resources and leveraging synergies between cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga, the deck can quickly establish a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan. Emry, Lurker of the Loch serves as both a valuable source of card advantage and a powerful recursion engine. The deck's explosiveness and versatility make it a formidable force in the Modern format.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively play low mana cost creatures and use instant and sorcery spells to pump them up and deal direct damage to the opponent. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, along with spells such as Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent and take control of the board. The inclusion of cards like Academic Dispute and Expressive Iteration allows for card draw and selection to keep the pressure on the opponent.
